heroSection.headline: Smart City
heroSection.paragraph: Chorn Planet studies how human movement, public space, mobility, food systems, and infrastructure signals can shape smarter city experiences from Chiang Mai to global audiences.

softTexts.title: Observe human flow
softTexts.description: Start with how people move, pause, gather, eat, work, and return home across the day.

softTexts.title: Translate signals into systems
softTexts.description: Connect visible city patterns to mobility, service, infrastructure, and platform decisions.

image.alt: People moving through a Chorn Planet Smart City environment

humanDailyFlow.headline: Human Daily Flow
humanDailyFlow.paragraph: A smart city becomes easier to understand when daily life is read as a sequence of human flows: morning movement, midday services, and evening return patterns.

morningFlow.title: Morning city movement
morningFlow.paragraph: Morning routines reveal how streets, transit edges, food stops, workplaces, and public space begin to synchronize before the city reaches full speed.

image.alt: Morning movement through a smart city district

middayFlow.title: Midday service rhythm
middayFlow.paragraph: Midday activity shows where people pause, order food, cross districts, use services, and turn ordinary convenience into measurable urban demand.

image.alt: Midday service rhythm in a smart city

eveningFlow.title: Evening return flow
eveningFlow.paragraph: Evening movement connects homes, restaurants, mobility links, and public comfort, showing how the city settles into a different pattern after work.

image.alt: Evening return flow through city streets

localToGlobal.headline: Local to Global
localToGlobal.paragraph: Chiang Mai can be observed as a local prototype for patterns that matter globally: walkability, tourism pressure, service timing, mobility corridors, and civic comfort.

items.title: Chiang Mai as a living prototype
items.description: Local streets, markets, campuses, old-city edges, and mountain connections create a practical lens for future city systems.

image.alt: Chiang Mai local life as a smart city prototype

items.title: Signals that travel
items.description: Patterns found in one city can inform wider thinking about service design, mobility planning, public comfort, and platform readiness.

image.alt: Local city signals translated into global patterns

systemExplainers.headline: System Explainers
systemExplainers.paragraph: Smart City content connects visible city life to the systems behind it, from mobility and services to data, comfort, and governance readiness.

systems.title: Movement and pause
systems.description: A city must support both efficient movement and meaningful places to stop.

image.alt: Movement and pause in a smart city system

systems.title: Density and comfort
systems.description: Useful density depends on shade, access, safety, service rhythm, and public-space quality.

image.alt: Density and comfort in an urban system

mobilityFocus.headline: Mobility Focus
mobilityFocus.paragraph: Mobility is treated as a city experience, not only a transport asset: the important question is how people move between homes, services, destinations, and public spaces.

signals.title: Corridors with purpose
signals.description: Mobility routes become more useful when they connect real daily needs rather than only technical infrastructure points.

image.alt: Smart mobility corridor connecting urban districts

signals.title: Airport and destination links
signals.description: Regional gateways, tourist movement, and local daily trips need to be understood together instead of as separate systems.

image.alt: Smart mobility airport connection for Chiang Mai

citySystems.headline: City Systems
citySystems.paragraph: Smart city value appears when separate signals become a coordinated system across movement, comfort, service timing, public space, and decision support.

systems.title: Efficiency and rhythm
systems.description: City systems should reduce friction while preserving the rhythm of local life and public space.

image.alt: Efficiency and rhythm in a smart city

systems.title: Movement and pause
systems.description: The same district needs routes for movement and comfortable places where people can stop, wait, meet, and recover.

image.alt: Movement and pause balanced in public space

systems.title: Density and comfort
systems.description: Dense environments work best when shade, access, safety, food, seating, and information are planned as one experience.

image.alt: Density and comfort in a city district

globalPatterns.headline: Global Patterns
globalPatterns.paragraph: The page turns local observation into reusable patterns for cities, platforms, and civic service design beyond one place.

signals.title: Human-first observation
signals.description: Start with the visible experience of people before choosing technology, dashboards, or infrastructure language.

image.alt: Human-first smart city observation

signals.title: Systems before technology
signals.description: Technology becomes useful when it supports a clear system of movement, services, information, and public comfort.

image.alt: Smart city systems before technology

urbanSignals.headline: Urban Signals
urbanSignals.paragraph: Urban signals are the practical clues that reveal whether a district is easy to move through, comfortable to stay in, and ready for smarter services.

signals.title: Queue and wait behavior
signals.description: Waiting patterns show where service capacity, shade, information, and pedestrian comfort need better coordination.

image.alt: Queue and wait behavior in public space

signals.title: Food and service demand
signals.description: Food stops and service touchpoints reveal how daily demand moves through neighborhoods and public corridors.

image.alt: Food and service demand in a smart city

signals.title: Public comfort signals
signals.description: Shade, seating, crossings, lighting, noise, and perceived safety determine whether people can use the city comfortably.

image.alt: Public comfort signals in a city district

editorialPositioning.headline: Editorial Positioning
paragraphs: Chorn Planet presents Smart City as an editorial and systems lens: observe daily life first, then explain what the city may need from mobility, services, public space, and technology.
The content avoids treating technology as the headline. It frames smart city thinking around human flow, local context, and practical system signals.

principles.title: Human life shapes the city
principles.description: People, routines, and local behavior are the starting point for every smart city interpretation.

image.alt: Human life shaping a smart city

principles.title: Local context matters
principles.description: A useful system respects the city that already exists before proposing future infrastructure.

image.alt: Local context in a smart city prototype

principles.title: Systems before technology
principles.description: Technology earns its place when the city system, user need, and operational role are clear.

image.alt: Systems before technology in smart city planning

hero.title: Smart City
paragraphs: Chorn Planet studies how human movement, public space, mobility, food systems, and infrastructure signals can shape smarter city experiences from Chiang Mai to global audiences.

image.alt: People moving through a Chorn Planet Smart City environment

items.title: Human Daily Flow
items.description: Read the city through morning, midday, and evening movement patterns.

items.title: City Systems
items.description: Connect mobility, density, service rhythm, comfort, and public space.

items.title: Editorial Lens
items.description: Understand why human-first observation comes before technology choices.

smartCityHighlight.title: Smart City Chiang Mai
smartCityHighlight.concept: Urban intelligence and future city planning
smartCityHighlight.description: Chorn Planet studies how human movement, public space, mobility, food systems, and infrastructure signals can shape smarter city experiences from Chiang Mai to global audiences.

features.title: Smart City direction
features.description: A public overview of smart city, mobility, and civic platform ideas for Chiang Mai.
